diagnosis: The level is a flat, linear corridor with no vertical traversal — spawn and exit sit on the same floor, and the only obstacles are repeating 6-tile platforms and 12-tile turret rows that the player can simply run under. The turrets fire horizontally from floor level, so their projectiles never reach the player's height, making them non-threats. The playtester completed 19.7 seconds with zero deaths, one hit, and one kill, confirming the level offers no meaningful challenge or engagement.
lessons: Stationary shooters placed on the same elevation as the player's movement path become irrelevant if the player can pass beneath their firing line — vertical offset or elevation change is required to make them threatening.
Repeating identical platform-and-turret patterns without variation in spacing, height, or timing create predictable, low-engagement encounters that players optimize away.
